confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_043fcb72-8038-4a9e-b33d-862bbb3bc597 read back as promoted

acceptance_shape: Narada docs/law contain the forward-momentum rule in agent-facing language.
Task lifecycle/review surfaces distinguish local scope completion from larger capability completion.
A future task like typed MCP facade closure would mechanically surface both Inbox MCP and EE-MCP continuations before closure is reported as done.

incident.missed_continuation: EE-MCP implementation task was not created alongside Inbox MCP continuation tasks 63 and 64 until Operator intervention.
incident.repair: Task 65 was created and Bob was nudged, but the process should have made this automatic at review/closure time.

proposed_capa: Add agent-facing law/doctrine: prototype, facade, spike, or partial-capability closure is a transition point, not an endpoint.
Require reviewer/architect to classify closure as scope-complete, capability-complete, doctrine-complete, or transition-complete.
When a task creates multiple typed surfaces or branches, require each branch to have one of: implementation task, explicit deferral with rationale, or explicit rejection.
Task review/close should warn when prototype/facade language appears and no continuation, deferral, or rejection is linked.
Agent role-loop indoctrination should emphasize preserving the full shape of a capability, not only the fragment most recently named by the Operator.
Forward-momentum behavior should be sanctioned: create the next coherent task and nudge the right role unless blocked or explicitly out of scope.
payload.requested_disposition: Treat as CAPA and doctrinal/process implementation proposal.
payload.root_cause_hypothesis: Agent operating posture over-optimized for local request satisfaction and scope closure, instead of inhabited forward motion over the whole typed capability shape.
payload.summary: During review of narada-andrey task 62, Kevin closed a typed MCP facade/prototype and initially created implementation continuation only for Inbox MCP, missing the sibling EE-MCP surface until the Operator challenged the narrowing. This shows an indoctrination/process gap: agents can satisfy the immediate wording while failing to preserve the full capability shape and forward momentum.
payload.title: CAPA: agents must treat prototype/facade closure as transition, not endpoint
